questions.stem: I ________ the Great Wall twice. It's really amazing and I want to visit it again.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。句中'twice'（两次）是表示经历的动作次数，是现在完成时的标志词。'have been to'表示'去过某地（已返回）'，符合语境；'have gone to'表示'去了某地（未回）'，与后文'想再去'矛盾；一般过去时'went'不能与twice搭配表示经历；'am going to'是将来时。解题技巧：看到表示次数的词语（once, twice, never等）优先考虑现在完成时have been to。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ________ is wearing a red jacket is my cousin. He just moved here last week.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词'the boy'指人，排除指物的which和不能引导定语从句的what。定语从句'____ is wearing a red jacket'缺少主语，who和whom都指人，但who作主语，whom作宾语。此处需要主格，故选who。解题技巧：指人用who/whom/that，指物用which/that；从句缺主语用who/which，缺宾语可用whom/who/which。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These books ________ to the school library tomorrow morning.
questions.explanation: 本题考查被动语态。主语'These books'与动词'return'是被动关系（书被归还），需用被动语态be+过去分词。时间状语'tomorrow morning'表示将来，因此用一般将来时的被动语态'will be returned'。A是主动语态原形，B是过去式，D是一般现在时被动，均不符合时间状语。解题技巧：先判断主被动（看主语是动作执行者还是承受者），再判断时态（看时间状语）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ________ be him. He has gone to Shanghai on business and won't be back until next Friday.
questions.explanation: 本题考查情态动词表推测。根据'He has gone to Shanghai'（他去上海了，未回）和'won't be back until next Friday'（下周五才回）可知，敲门的'不可能'是他。can't表示'不可能'，用于否定推测；must表示'一定是'（肯定推测）；might表示'可能是'（不确定）；should表示'应该是'。解题技巧：has gone to表示去了未回，看到这种情况要注意是否定推测的信号。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: We should ________ good habits of reading every day. It helps us learn better and opens up our minds.
questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。'develop habits'是固定搭配，意为'养成习惯'。take表示'拿、花费'；make表示'制作'；grow表示'生长'，虽然中文有时说'养成'，但英文中habits与develop搭配。解题技巧：记住常见搭配develop/form good habits（养成好习惯），get into bad habits（沾染坏习惯）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The task is too difficult. I think it is ________ for a 10-year-old child to finish it alone in such a short time.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词义辨析和语境理解。前句说'too difficult'（太难），因此推断对10岁孩子来说'不可能'完成。impossible表示'不可能的'；possible表示'可能的'，与语境相反；easy表示'容易的'，也与语境相反；necessary表示'必要的'，不符合逻辑。解题技巧：注意前句的提示词too difficult，这是解题关键。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The good news made everyone ________. They all smiled happily and cheered loudly.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词性转换和make的用法。'make sb. + adj.'是固定结构，表示'使某人……'，需要形容词作宾补。happily是副词，happiness是名词，均不符合语法；unhappy（不高兴的）与后文'smiled happily'矛盾；happy（高兴的）符合语境。解题技巧：make/keep/find/think+宾语+形容词（作宾补），这是初中重点结构。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。语境是'找出减少浪费的方法'。find out强调经过调查、研究发现、弄清楚；look for强调寻找的动作；search后常接for；make up表示'编造、组成'。此处指'查明、找出'方法，find out最恰当。解题技巧：find out侧重于找到抽象的答案、方法、真相；look for侧重于寻找具体物品。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project about environmental protection. We were asked to (8)______ ways to reduce waste in our school. At first, I thought it would be boring, but later I found it quite (9)______.

My group decided to (10)______ on paper recycling. We found that tons of paper (11)______ away every semester. To solve this problem, we set up recycling boxes in each classroom. We also made posters to tell students the (12)______ of saving paper.

The project lasted for three weeks. When we presented our results to the whole school, everyone was surprised by the changes we had made. Now our school saves about 30% paper each month. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.explanation: 本题考查情感线索和转折关系。前句说'起初我以为会很无聊'，but表示转折，因此后句应表达相反的情感，即'有趣的'。boring（无聊的）与转折矛盾；difficult（困难的）和necessary（必要的）不能形成与boring的对比转折。interesting（有趣的）符合逻辑。解题技巧：看到but/however等转折词，前后语义通常相反。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project about environmental protection. We were asked to (8)______ ways to reduce waste in our school. At first, I thought it would be boring, but later I found it quite (9)______.

My group decided to (10)______ on paper recycling. We found that tons of paper (11)______ away every semester. To solve this problem, we set up recycling boxes in each classroom. We also made posters to tell students the (12)______ of saving paper.

The project lasted for three weeks. When we presented our results to the whole school, everyone was surprised by the changes we had made. Now our school saves about 30% paper each month. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配。'focus on'是固定短语，意为'专注于、集中于'，符合语境'我们小组决定专注于纸张回收'。look on表示'旁观'；carry on表示'继续'；put on表示'穿上'，均不符合语境。解题技巧：记住常见短语focus on...（专注于……），concentrate on...（集中于……）。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project about environmental protection. We were asked to (8)______ ways to reduce waste in our school. At first, I thought it would be boring, but later I found it quite (9)______.

My group decided to (10)______ on paper recycling. We found that tons of paper (11)______ away every semester. To solve this problem, we set up recycling boxes in each classroom. We also made posters to tell students the (12)______ of saving paper.

The project lasted for three weeks. When we presented our results to the whole school, everyone was surprised by the changes we had made. Now our school saves about 30% paper each month. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.explanation: 本题考查被动语态和主谓一致。paper与throw away（扔掉）是被动关系，且tons of paper（数吨纸）是复数概念，因此用were thrown。was thrown用于单数；have thrown和threw都是主动语态。解题技巧：tons of+名词，谓语动词与tons保持一致，用复数；paper是不可数名词，但tons是复数。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project about environmental protection. We were asked to (8)______ ways to reduce waste in our school. At first, I thought it would be boring, but later I found it quite (9)______.

My group decided to (10)______ on paper recycling. We found that tons of paper (11)______ away every semester. To solve this problem, we set up recycling boxes in each classroom. We also made posters to tell students the (12)______ of saving paper.

The project lasted for three weeks. When we presented our results to the whole school, everyone was surprised by the changes we had made. Now our school saves about 30% paper each month. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.explanation: 本题考查名词辨析和语境理解。制作海报是为了告诉学生节约用纸的'重要性'，the importance of...是常用搭配。way和method都表示'方法'，但此处是宣传节约用纸的意义而非方法；result（结果）不符合语境。解题技巧：the importance of...（……的重要性）是初中完形高频考点。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project about environmental protection. We were asked to (8)______ ways to reduce waste in our school. At first, I thought it would be boring, but later I found it quite (9)______.

My group decided to (10)______ on paper recycling. We found that tons of paper (11)______ away every semester. To solve this problem, we set up recycling boxes in each classroom. We also made posters to tell students the (12)______ of saving paper.

The project lasted for three weeks. When we presented our results to the whole school, everyone was surprised by the changes we had made. Now our school saves about 30% paper each month. This experience taught me that small actions can make a big difference.

questions.stem: What can smart lights do according to the passage?
options: A. They can clean the room automatically.
B. They turn on when someone enters and adjust brightness by time.
C. They only work during the daytime.
D. They can control the room temperature.
questions.explanation: 本题是细节理解题。根据第二段第二句'smart lights can turn on automatically when you enter a room and adjust their brightness based on the time of day'可知，智能灯可以在人进入房间时自动开启，并根据一天中的时间调整亮度。A选项'清洁房间'文中未提；C选项'只在白天工作'与原文相反；D选项'控制室温'是恒温器的功能。解题技巧：细节题要精准定位原文，找到关键词smart lights所在句子，比对选项与原文的同义替换。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Imagine living in a house that knows you better than you know yourself. This is not science fiction—it's the reality of smart homes.

Smart homes use advanced technology to make our lives easier and more comfortable. For example, smart lights can turn on automatically when you enter a room and adjust their brightness based on the time of day. Smart thermostats (恒温器) learn your daily routine and set the perfect temperature before you even get home.

However, smart homes are not just about convenience. They also help save energy. When no one is in the house, the system turns off unnecessary lights and appliances (电器). Studies show that smart homes can reduce energy use by up to 30%.

Despite these benefits, some people worry about privacy. Smart home devices collect data about our daily habits. If this information falls into the wrong hands, it could be dangerous. Therefore, it is important to choose trustworthy brands and regularly update passwords.

As technology continues to advance, smart homes will become more common. Perhaps in the near future, everyone will live in a house that takes care of them.

questions.stem: What can we learn about smart homes from the passage?
options: A. They are only popular with young people.
B. They will replace traditional homes completely next year.
C. They may have privacy risks that users should pay attention to.
D. They are too expensive for most families to afford.
questions.explanation: 本题是推理判断题。根据第四段'some people worry about privacy...If this information falls into the wrong hands, it could be dangerous'可推断，智能家居可能存在隐私风险，用户需要注意。A选项'只受年轻人欢迎'文中未提；B选项'明年完全取代传统住宅'过于绝对且时间错误；D选项'太贵'文中未提价格。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能过度推断，也不能选择文中明确未提及的内容。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Imagine living in a house that knows you better than you know yourself. This is not science fiction—it's the reality of smart homes.

Smart homes use advanced technology to make our lives easier and more comfortable. For example, smart lights can turn on automatically when you enter a room and adjust their brightness based on the time of day. Smart thermostats (恒温器) learn your daily routine and set the perfect temperature before you even get home.

However, smart homes are not just about convenience. They also help save energy. When no one is in the house, the system turns off unnecessary lights and appliances (电器). Studies show that smart homes can reduce energy use by up to 30%.

Despite these benefits, some people worry about privacy. Smart home devices collect data about our daily habits. If this information falls into the wrong hands, it could be dangerous. Therefore, it is important to choose trustworthy brands and regularly update passwords.

As technology continues to advance, smart homes will become more common. Perhaps in the near future, everyone will live in a house that takes care of them.

questions.stem: The underlined word 'adjust' in Paragraph 2 probably means ________.
questions.explanation: 本题是词义猜测题。根据上下文'adjust their brightness based on the time of day'（根据一天中的时间____亮度），可推测adjust意为'调整、改变'。change（改变）最接近；break（打破）、fix（修理）、buy（购买）均不符合语境。解题技巧：通过上下文线索猜测词义，brightness（亮度）和time of day（时间）提示是对亮度进行调节，即改变亮度。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Imagine living in a house that knows you better than you know yourself. This is not science fiction—it's the reality of smart homes.

Smart homes use advanced technology to make our lives easier and more comfortable. For example, smart lights can turn on automatically when you enter a room and adjust their brightness based on the time of day. Smart thermostats (恒温器) learn your daily routine and set the perfect temperature before you even get home.

However, smart homes are not just about convenience. They also help save energy. When no one is in the house, the system turns off unnecessary lights and appliances (电器). Studies show that smart homes can reduce energy use by up to 30%.

Despite these benefits, some people worry about privacy. Smart home devices collect data about our daily habits. If this information falls into the wrong hands, it could be dangerous. Therefore, it is important to choose trustworthy brands and regularly update passwords.

As technology continues to advance, smart homes will become more common. Perhaps in the near future, everyone will live in a house that takes care of them.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Smart Homes
B. How to Save Energy at Home
C. Smart Homes: Convenient but Challenging
D. The Dangers of Modern Technology
questions.explanation: 本题是主旨大意题。文章既介绍了智能家居的便利（第二段）和节能（第三段），也讨论了隐私问题（第四段），因此C选项'智能家居：便利但有挑战'最全面。A选项'历史'文中未提；B选项'如何节能'只是部分内容；D选项'现代技术的危险'过于宽泛且片面。解题技巧：最佳标题要涵盖全文主要内容，既不能太宽泛也不能太片面，要体现文章的核心话题和作者的态度倾向。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Imagine living in a house that knows you better than you know yourself. This is not science fiction—it's the reality of smart homes.

Smart homes use advanced technology to make our lives easier and more comfortable. For example, smart lights can turn on automatically when you enter a room and adjust their brightness based on the time of day. Smart thermostats (恒温器) learn your daily routine and set the perfect temperature before you even get home.

However, smart homes are not just about convenience. They also help save energy. When no one is in the house, the system turns off unnecessary lights and appliances (电器). Studies show that smart homes can reduce energy use by up to 30%.

Despite these benefits, some people worry about privacy. Smart home devices collect data about our daily habits. If this information falls into the wrong hands, it could be dangerous. Therefore, it is important to choose trustworthy brands and regularly update passwords.

As technology continues to advance, smart homes will become more common. Perhaps in the near future, everyone will live in a house that takes care of them.

options: A. However, it requires a lot of money to get started.
B. First, save energy and resources in your daily life.
C. Second, choose green transportation whenever possible.
D. For example, stop using single-use plastics.
E. Finally, share your ideas with others.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接和顺序词。根据文章结构，这里开始列举步骤，后文有Start with small things（从小事做起），因此这里应该是第一步。B选项'First...'（首先……）符合逻辑顺序。A选项However表转折，与上文衔接不当；C选项Second（第二）应该出现在First之后；D选项For example（例如）用于举例，不适合作为开头；E选项Finally（最后）用于结尾。解题技巧：注意文章中的顺序词first/second/third/finally，它们通常按顺序出现。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Living a green life is not as hard as you think. Here are some simple steps to help protect our planet.

(17)______ Start with small things like turning off lights when leaving a room or using both sides of paper. These little actions add up to big changes over time.

(18)______ Instead of driving to school every day, try riding a bike or walking. This not only reduces air pollution but also keeps you fit and healthy.

Another important step is to reduce waste. (19)______ Bring your own shopping bags when going to the supermarket. Choose products with less packaging. When shopping for clothes, ask yourself if you really need them.

(20)______ Talk to your friends and family about environmental protection. The more people join in, the bigger difference we can make.

options: A. However, it requires a lot of money to get started.
B. First, save energy and resources in your daily life.
C. Second, choose green transportation whenever possible.
D. For example, stop using single-use plastics.
E. Finally, share your ideas with others.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接和顺序词。前文有First，后文内容关于'instead of driving...riding a bike or walking'（不开车，骑车或步行），即绿色出行方式。C选项'Second, choose green transportation...'（第二，尽可能选择绿色出行）正好承接上文并引出下文。解题技巧：关注段落间的逻辑顺序，first之后通常是second，内容要与段落中的例子相符。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Living a green life is not as hard as you think. Here are some simple steps to help protect our planet.

(17)______ Start with small things like turning off lights when leaving a room or using both sides of paper. These little actions add up to big changes over time.

(18)______ Instead of driving to school every day, try riding a bike or walking. This not only reduces air pollution but also keeps you fit and healthy.

Another important step is to reduce waste. (19)______ Bring your own shopping bags when going to the supermarket. Choose products with less packaging. When shopping for clothes, ask yourself if you really need them.

(20)______ Talk to your friends and family about environmental protection. The more people join in, the bigger difference we can make.

options: A. However, it requires a lot of money to get started.
B. First, save energy and resources in your daily life.
C. Second, choose green transportation whenever possible.
D. For example, stop using single-use plastics.
E. Finally, share your ideas with others.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接和举例关系。前句说'Another important step is to reduce waste'（另一个重要步骤是减少浪费），后句说'Bring your own shopping bags...'（自带购物袋……）。D选项'For example, stop using single-use plastics'（例如，停止使用一次性塑料）承上启下，举例说明如何减少浪费，且与后文的购物袋、包装等内容一致。解题技巧：for example/instance通常用于提出具体例子，证明前文的观点。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Living a green life is not as hard as you think. Here are some simple steps to help protect our planet.

(17)______ Start with small things like turning off lights when leaving a room or using both sides of paper. These little actions add up to big changes over time.

(18)______ Instead of driving to school every day, try riding a bike or walking. This not only reduces air pollution but also keeps you fit and healthy.

Another important step is to reduce waste. (19)______ Bring your own shopping bags when going to the supermarket. Choose products with less packaging. When shopping for clothes, ask yourself if you really need them.

(20)______ Talk to your friends and family about environmental protection. The more people join in, the bigger difference we can make.

options: A. However, it requires a lot of money to get started.
B. First, save energy and resources in your daily life.
C. Second, choose green transportation whenever possible.
D. For example, stop using single-use plastics.
E. Finally, share your ideas with others.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接和顺序词。这是文章最后一段，应该使用表示总结或最后的过渡词。E选项'Finally, share your ideas with others'（最后，与他人分享你的想法）符合逻辑顺序，且后文'Talk to your friends and family...'（与朋友和家人交谈）正是分享想法的具体表现。解题技巧：finally/last/in the end通常用于列举的最后一项，表示总结或建议的终结。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Living a green life is not as hard as you think. Here are some simple steps to help protect our planet.

(17)______ Start with small things like turning off lights when leaving a room or using both sides of paper. These little actions add up to big changes over time.

(18)______ Instead of driving to school every day, try riding a bike or walking. This not only reduces air pollution but also keeps you fit and healthy.

Another important step is to reduce waste. (19)______ Bring your own shopping bags when going to the supermarket. Choose products with less packaging. When shopping for clothes, ask yourself if you really need them.

(20)______ Talk to your friends and family about environmental protection. The more people join in, the bigger difference we can make.
